Regression test for issue 1083 in JerryScript, using various try-catch blocks with JavaScript objects.

# Purpose
This code is a series of `try-catch` blocks that test the behavior of JavaScript error handling. Each `try` block contains code that is likely to throw an error, such as accessing properties on undefined objects or invoking non-existent methods. The `catch` blocks capture any exceptions thrown during the execution of the `try` blocks, using the variable `A` to handle the caught exceptions. This script is useful for testing the robustness of error handling in JavaScript environments, ensuring that exceptions are properly caught and managed without causing the program to crash. The code does not perform any specific functionality beyond testing error handling mechanisms.
